title: Iconic Fugazzeta and Anchovy Pizza Slices on Avenida Corrientes in Buenos Aires City Argentina
description: Buenos Aires, Buenos Aires: A close-up, high-angle composition captures the rich, greasy textures of quintessential Porteño-style pizza slices served on a traditional white plate. The left slice features a vibrant, thick tomato base topped with salty, preserved anchovies, while the right section showcases the heavy, molten cheese characteristic of local fugazzeta, garnished with thin-sliced onions and a bright red pepper sliver. Warm, interior restaurant lighting reflects off the oily surface of the dough, creating a shallow depth of field that draws the eye toward the distinct culinary layers. Audrey Bergner’s hand enters the frame from the upper left, providing a sense of scale and intimate dining participation as the meal is about to be enjoyed. The composition emphasizes the contrast between the deep, rustic red of the tomato sauce and the creamy, pale aesthetic of the cheese-heavy slices. This scene represents a fundamental pillar of Argentine urban dining culture, where late-night pizzeria hopping along the famous Avenida Corrientes is a quintessential social experience. By documenting these local culinary staples, the image serves as an authentic look into the gastronomic identity that defines the nightlife and communal table culture of this sprawling metropolis. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
